event_name: @cyclonedx/cdxgen Maven scanning command injection via repository-controlled module paths
confidence_reason: Direct upstream GitHub Security Advisory and upstream pull request identify affected package, vulnerable range, fixed release, and the Maven scanner command-construction flaw.

credentials_at_risk: developer workstation credentials visible to scanner process

artifact_analysis.execution_trigger: Scanning an attacker-controlled Maven project with @cyclonedx/cdxgen before 12.4.3
payload_behavior: repository-controlled path interpreted by shell during Maven scanner command construction

open_questions: No reviewed direct source confirmed exploitation in the wild as of the 2026-06-26 advisory review.
The public advisory does not quantify how many downstream CI systems scanned untrusted Maven projects with affected cdxgen versions.

defender_takeaways.detection: Search source, lockfile, and CI evidence for @cyclonedx/cdxgen before 12.4.3 and cdxgen Maven scanner invocations.
defender_takeaways.hunting: Prioritize SBOM jobs that scan pull requests, forks, or third-party Maven repositories.
defender_takeaways.remediation: Upgrade cdxgen to 12.4.3 or later before scanning untrusted Maven projects.
defender_takeaways.prevention: Run SBOM generation for untrusted repositories in isolated runners without long-lived credentials.

containment_complete: Affected SBOM jobs are disabled or moved to isolated runners until cdxgen is upgraded.
eradication_complete: Lockfiles and scanner containers show @cyclonedx/cdxgen 12.4.3 or later.
recovery_complete: Recent SBOM job logs have been reviewed for unexpected command execution indicators.
closure_complete: All untrusted-repository scanning paths have documented runner isolation and patched cdxgen versions.
